subject: Biology
title: Cells Under the Microscope
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Cells Under the Microscope.

hook.text: In the 1670s, van Leeuwenhoek ground his own lenses and saw living things in a water drop.

buildup.text: He called them 'animalcules.' No one had seen single-celled life before.

discovery.text: Light microscopes reveal cells but can't resolve anything smaller than 200 nanometers.

twist.text: Electron microscopes shattered that limit, showing organelles in stunning detail.

climax.text: Modern cryo-EM can image individual proteins at near-atomic resolution.

punchline.text: Better lenses keep revealing smaller worlds.

quiz.question: What did van Leeuwenhoek call the organisms he saw under his microscope?
